_comment: Evals for the manual, opt-in coverage skill. Each prompt invokes the skill against a Spring Boot / Gradle target. To run the full benchmark loop a real Java target with coverage gaps is required (e.g. a throwaway clone of a a backend service); the with-seam / no-seam fixtures here drive the command-resolution cases. Expectations are written to be objectively checkable from the run transcript + produced outputs.

evals.prompt: Use the sonarqube-test-coverage skill to raise coverage on the module-server module before I open my PR. The project's .claude/config_hints.json is the one provided.
evals.expected_output: The skill resolves the coverage command from config_hints.coverage.command and runs exactly that command; it does not invent a different gradle invocation or guess a report path.
expectations: Reads config_hints.json and resolves the coverage command from the coverage.command seam
Runs the exact command from coverage.command (./gradlew testCodeCoverageReport --rerun-tasks --no-daemon) rather than a guessed or different invocation
Locates the report at the coverage.report_glob path rather than searching blindly
Does not derive a command from test_command when coverage.command is present

evals.prompt: Run sonarqube-test-coverage on my staged changes so I know what Sonar would flag as uncovered new code.
evals.expected_output: Every coverage run (initial and the re-run in the verify phase) bypasses the build cache; the skill explicitly forces a fresh run rather than trusting a cached report.
expectations: Resolves the scope from staged changes via git diff --cached --name-only and filters to production source files only
Every coverage invocation includes the cache-bypass flag (--rerun-tasks) or otherwise forces a fresh run
States or demonstrates that a cached coverage report would be stale and must not be trusted
Re-runs coverage with the cache bypassed in the verify phase, not just the first time

evals.prompt: I have a bunch of changes on this branch. Use sonarqube-test-coverage to close the coverage gaps for the diff against origin/main...HEAD — I only care about the lines this PR actually adds or changes.
evals.expected_output: The skill intersects the report's uncovered/partial lines with the diff so only newly added or modified lines are in scope; pre-existing untested code is reported as out of scope, not targeted for new tests.
expectations: Cross-references uncovered/partial lines with git diff --unified=0 origin/main...HEAD per file
Only newly added or modified lines are treated as in-scope coverage gaps
Pre-existing uncovered lines outside the diff are explicitly excluded, not written tests for
Classifies lines correctly (not-covered when mi>0 && ci==0; partial when mi>0 && ci>0; branch-gap when mb>0) using the report attributes

evals.prompt: Use sonarqube-test-coverage to get PricingService.java to full coverage. Heads up: it has a defensive `throw new IllegalStateException` branch that only fires on an input the public API can't actually produce.
evals.expected_output: The skill writes meaningful tests for reachable lines, caps iteration at 3 passes, and flags the unreachable defensive branch under 'Remaining gaps' with a reason rather than contorting a test to hit it.
expectations: Stops iterating after at most 3 coverage passes
Flags the unreachable defensive branch under a Remaining gaps section with a stated reason
Does not use reflection, visibility hacks, or contrived inputs to force coverage of the unreachable line
Tests written for reachable lines assert behavior, not just execute lines for coverage

evals.prompt: Get my new mapper and service up to the coverage gate with sonarqube-test-coverage and commit it for me.
evals.expected_output: The skill writes the unit tests but does NOT commit or push (it defers committing to the caller / commit skill), and it does NOT add or modify integration tests.
expectations: Writes unit tests in the test tree mirroring the production package
Does not run git commit or git push
States that committing is the caller's responsibility (e.g. via the commit skill with user approval)
Does not create or modify any test under an integration-tests module

evals.prompt: Run sonarqube-test-coverage on the module-server module. The provided .claude/config_hints.json is what this project has.
evals.expected_output: With no coverage seam present, the skill falls back in order — installed unit-testing rule, then derive from test_command — and explicitly states the assumption it made about the coverage command and report path instead of silently guessing.
expectations: Detects that config_hints has no coverage.command seam
Falls back to the project's installed unit-testing rule for the coverage command/report path, or derives from test_command if the rule is absent
Explicitly states the assumption it made about the coverage command and report location
Still bypasses the build cache in whatever command it derives
